title: REST with GraphQL
excerpt: Hi! I need someone to help me set up an integration between Companies House (REST API) with Monday. What I need it to pull is not particularly complicated, but I think I need some assistance setting up the link between R…

posts.author: kescott (Katie Escott)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! I need someone to help me set up an integration between Companies House (REST API) with Monday. What I need it to pull is not particularly complicated, but I think I need some assistance setting up the link between REST and GraphQL.

posts.author: Fruition (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @kescott

Josh here from Fruition and ex-monday employee, we’re Monday.com partner certified, specialise in API integration and Monday consulting services.

posts.author: DamcoSolutions ( Neelam Bhatt)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @kescott

Fortunately, the monday .com community has launched an exclusive API software development kit (SDK). This will help you to link and query the GraphQL API impeccably on behalf of the connected user or by utilizing an API token. Resultant scopes will be based on the authorizations of the logged-in operator and the preferences you have configured in your app. To make using monday .com API easier, they have provided documentation explaining the queries and mutations for users. You can also run your queries in the virtual GraphQL explorer.

If you’re seeking assistance in setting up the link between REST and GraphQL, collaborating with a monday.com consulting services provider is the perfect option. Their dedicated developers can set up connections as per your preference.
